title: helianthemum oleifolium
intro: Helianthemum oleifolium, also known as Rockrose or Frostweed, is a perennial shrub native to North America, characterized by its small, pink or white flowers and edible leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Helianthemum oleifolium include relief from colds and fever, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The herbal preparations of Rockrose typically involve infusions, decoctions, or salves made from its leaves and flowers, often used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
